digests.title: TLDR Daily Digest
digests.tagline: Tech, AI, IT, Web Dev, InfoSec, DevOps, Design

stories.headline: OpenAI releases GPT-5.6 with Sol, Terra, Luna and multi-agent coordination
stories.blurb: The three-tier model lineup offers frontier intelligence at lower token costs, with Sol achieving the first ARC-AGI-3 win and introducing an 'ultra' multi-agent setting for complex tasks.

stories.headline: Meta launches Muse Spark 1.1 with aggressive API pricing
stories.blurb: Meta's new model tier charges 25% of competing API prices for state-of-the-art agentic reasoning and tool use, with a free tier up to a usage limit.

stories.headline: Apple explores running 27B-parameter models directly on iPhones
stories.blurb: Apple is testing PrismML's technology to run large AI models like Qwen 3.6 locally on iPhone Pro, enabling more on-device Apple Intelligence features with better privacy.

stories.headline: OpenAI accused of hiding evidence in copyright case, faces possible sanctions
stories.blurb: A privacy engineer's revelation that OpenAI misled the court about searching ChatGPT logs could lead to serious penalties in the lawsuit with news organizations.

stories.headline: npm 12 disables install scripts by default to curb supply chain attacks
stories.blurb: The new version requires explicit allowlisting for install scripts, git dependencies, and remote URL installs, significantly reducing attack surface for the JavaScript ecosystem.

stories.headline: Microsoft patches RoguePlanet zero-day in Defender after public dispute
stories.blurb: The Malware Protection Engine update fixes a race condition granting SYSTEM privileges, weeks after exploit code was published and following public clashes with researcher Nightmare Eclipse.

stories.headline: Discord AI moderation bug wrongfully bans 8,000 users in 24 hours
stories.blurb: A glitch in Discord's new AI content model targeted geometric images, causing mass bans and a 40x spike in appeals, highlighting the fragility of automated moderation.

stories.headline: Proof-of-concept turns defensive AI agents into remote code execution vectors
stories.blurb: Researchers demonstrated that prompt injection in a tampered open-source library can trick Claude Code and Codex into running malicious scripts during security reviews.

stories.headline: GitHub automates repository ownership at scale across its internal org
stories.blurb: GitHub assigned validated owners to every active repository in its primary organization within 45 days, archiving unowned ones and making ownership the foundation for security.

stories.headline: PostHog shares pipeline for fast code review of AI-generated code
stories.blurb: PostHog built a workflow that delegates review to agents, removing humans as bottleneck while maintaining quality, with human oversight only for critical changes.

stories.headline: Bun rewrites its runtime from Zig to Rust for better ecosystem support
stories.blurb: The JavaScript runtime is migrating to Rust for high-level code while keeping Zig for JavaScriptCore bindings, citing safer concurrency and stronger ecosystem.

stories.headline: etcd v3.7.0 ships RangeStream, reducing latency and memory for large queries
stories.blurb: The new feature streams large results in chunks, improving Kubernetes control plane CPU usage and completing the removal of legacy v2 store dependencies.

stories.headline: IBM and Red Hat launch Lightwell products to secure open-source from AI attacks
stories.blurb: Lightwell Network and Clearinghouse Premier use AI-driven remediation and backports to deliver validated security fixes for open-source dependencies, keeping fixes upstream.

stories.headline: JetBrains unifies AI-assisted development with a governance suite for enterprises
stories.blurb: The new layer centralizes management of multiple AI coding tools within JetBrains IDEs, providing shared context, cost controls, usage visibility, and compliance.
